name: French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop from Ponferrada
name_original: Las Puentes de Malpaso – circular desde Ponferrada por el Camino Francés
description: This 21.2-mile mountain biking loop from Ponferrada combines the French Way and Puentes de Malpaso, offering a challenging ride with histori
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ponferrada offers diverse terrain within the El Bierzo region of Castile and León. The area is characterized by surrounding mountain ranges, including the Montes de León and Montes Aquilianos, which provide varied elevations and challenging descents. The fertile El Bierzo basin and the Sil River contribute to a landscape of valleys, forests, and reservoirs. This geographical setting creates an extensive network of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Ponferrada The most popular mountain bike trail is Monte Pajariel Loop from Ponferrada, a 19.1 mile (30.8 km) moderate route that takes about 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This trail offers technical descents and flow sections. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Explorers and Quarrymen Path, Bárcena Reservoir and Pilgrims Path – circular route through Ponferrada, a moderate 20.7 mile (33.2 km) path. This route passes by the Bárcena Reservoir and incorporates sections of the historic Pilgrims Path. Local mountain bikers also love the Peñas de la Riestra de Congosto – circular desde Ponferrada por el Embalse de Bárcena, a 27.9 mile (45.0 km) trail leading through the area around the Embalse de Bárcena, often completed in about 6 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Ponferrada is defined by the Montes Aquilianos, the El Bierzo basin, and the Sil River. The network offers options for easy family rides to challenging enduro descents. The routes in Ponferrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1,300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ponferrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ponferrada?
A: Ponferrada and the surrounding El Bierzo region offer a vast network of mountain bike trails. On komoot, you'll find over 130 routes, catering to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging enduro descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ponferrada?
A: Yes, there are several trails ideal for beginners. Out of the 139 routes listed on komoot, 17 are classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the region. These routes often feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ponferrada?
A: The terrain around Ponferrada is incredibly diverse, thanks to its location within the El Bierzo region, surrounded by the Montes de León and Montes Aquilianos. You can expect everything from fertile basin valleys and forest paths to challenging mountain ascents and technical descents. The area also features routes along the Sil River and around the Bárcena Reservoir.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Ponferrada offers 27 difficult mountain bike routes. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and technical sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036711359/el-ferradillo-campo-de-las-danzas-la-guiana-12-apostoles-y-montes-de-valdueza-circular-desde-san-esteban-de-valdueza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Ferradillo, Campo de las Danzas, La Guiana, 12 Apóstoles y Montes de Valdueza – circular desde San Esteban de Valdueza&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 45 km with over 1,400 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the Ponferrada area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Ponferrada are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036677796/french-way-and-puentes-de-malpaso-loop-from-ponferrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop from Ponferrada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34 km route that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions I can see while mountain biking near Ponferrada?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. While mountain biking, you might encounter stunning views of the Montes de León, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montes de León&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also close to the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Las Médulas, with its unique red cliffs, and the tranquil Valle del Silencio. Some routes also pass by significant points on the Camino de Santiago, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ponferrada?
A: Ponferrada benefits from a milder climate due to its location in the El Bierzo basin. While mountain biking is possible for much of the year, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winter might bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations in the Montes Aquilianos.

Q: How do mountain bike trails in Ponferrada compare to the Camino de Santiago routes?
A: Ponferrada is a significant stop on the French Way of the Camino de Santiago. While some mountain bike routes may intersect or run parallel to sections of the Camino, dedicated mountain bike trails often venture into more technical and challenging terrain in the surrounding mountains, offering a different experience than the generally less technical Camino paths. However, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036241143/explorers-and-quarrymen-path-barcena-reservoir-and-pilgrims-path-circular-route-through-ponferrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Explorers and Quarrymen Path, Bárcena Reservoir and Pilgrims Path&lt;/a&gt;, do incorporate parts of the historic Pilgrims Path.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ponferrada?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ponferrada are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural scenery, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels. Over 1,300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore this region.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by reservoirs or rivers?
A: Yes, the landscape around Ponferrada includes the Sil River and the Bárcena Reservoir. Several routes incorporate these features, offering scenic rides along the water. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036511682/penas-de-la-riestra-de-congosto-circular-desde-ponferrada-por-el-embalse-de-barcena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas de la Riestra de Congosto – circular desde Ponferrada por el Embalse de Bárcena&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that leads through the area around the Embalse de Bárcena.
tips: Beautiful and technical double and single track that runs between Riego de Ambrós and Molinaseca. One of the picturesque paths that pilgrims travel during the French Way that offers us beautiful views.
Popular 1km double track trail near the town of Riego de Ambrós. It can be done in both directions but we have to be careful, since it is a passage area of the French Way.

Los Puentes de Malpaso is one of the many wonders that we find for lovers of cycling and technical descents in the El Bierzo region. A "single track" path of more than 5 kilometers that runs between Riego de Ambrós and Molinaseca next to the Meruelo river. During the descent we will come across two stone bridges that cross the river and that are worth stopping to see even if you are in the middle of a hectic descent.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 34.1 km mountain bike loop is around 3 hours and 20 minutes of active riding time. However, this can vary based on your fitness level, technical skills, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or navigate challenging sections.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is generally considered a moderate mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. The Puentes de Malpaso Trail section, in particular, is rated as 'Red Rate' difficulty, featuring steep, technical parts, stone steps, and potential drops that may require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where does the French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Ponferrada, offering convenient logistics. As it's a circular route from a town, you can typically find parking within Ponferrada itself, though specific trailhead parking might require checking local maps or signs upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, combining regional roads, dirt tracks, and significant singletrack sections, especially along the Meruelo River in the Puentes de Malpaso area. You'll encounter stony paths, steep ascents, descents, and challenging stone steps. Muddy conditions can also be present, adding to the technical difficulty.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and picturesque scenery, traversing the 'green valley of El Bierzo' with views of mountainous landscapes. The route passes through charming traditional stone villages like Riego de Ambros and Molinaseca. The historic Puentes de Malpaso themselves are notable stone bridges over the Meruelo River. High points along the Camino Francés can offer spectacular panoramic views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the trails are less likely to be excessively hot or muddy. Summer can be very warm, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations, making some sections more challenging or impassable.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or landmarks I'll encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include sections of the historic Camino Francés, particularly the stretch from Riego de Ambrós to Molinaseca. The ancient Puentes de Malpaso, with their Roman gold extraction history, are a significant feature. You'll also pass through traditional villages like Riego de Ambrós and Molinaseca.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates sections of several other trails. Notably, it follows parts of the Camino Francés - 06 León a Cacabelos, Las Puentes de Malpaso, and the Camino Natural La Mirada Circular. You'll also encounter smaller segments of the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas) and [CIMA LE02] Foncebadón - Cruz de Ferro * Molinaseca.

faqs.question: Is the French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on many trails in the region, the technical and challenging nature of this specific mountain bike loop, especially the Puentes de Malpaso Trail with its stone steps and narrow sections, might not be ideal for all dogs, particularly if they are not accustomed to such terrain or if you need to dismount frequently. Always keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of other trail users, especially pilgrims on the Camino sections.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the French Way and Puentes de Malpaso Loop. It uses public trails and sections of the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling the challenging Puentes de Malpaso section?
faqs.answer: The Puentes de Malpaso Trail is the most technical part, rated 'Red Rate'. Be prepared for steep sections, difficult stone steps, and narrow passages. It's wise to anticipate needing to dismount and push or even carry your bike in certain spots. Approach this section with caution and allow extra time for navigation.

waypoints.name: Riego de Ambrós to Molinaseca - Camino Francés
